item.title: Pneumonia leading cause of death after measles
item.description: Specialist physicians at a press conference on Friday said that pneumonia had now emerged as a major complication of measles, appearing as one of the leading causes of death among measles patients....

item.title: Common river water fair share still a far cry
item.description: Today marks the 50th anniversary of the historic Farakka Long March, renewing Bangladesh’s call for a fair share of water from the transboundary rivers shared with India....

item.title: Farmers falling in debt trap, food supply may get hurt
item.description: Fifty-year-old farmer Sudhin Chandra Das cultivated boro paddy on 150 decimals of leased land in Shalla upazila of Sunamganj, part of the country’s haor region, hoping to harvest the crop in late April....

item.title: Lack of govt support for publishing sector resented
item.description: Speakers, including academics, writers, publishers, and researchers, said on Friday that developed countries invested heavily in both education and public publishing infrastructure....

item.title: RCC installs traditional Dhopkol
item.description: The Rajshahi City Corporation installed a traditional Dhopkol, water hydrant, at the Sikiribona rail crossing as part of an initiative to preserve the city’s cultural and historical heritage....

item.title: Forty years after Chernobyl, same mistakes persist
item.description: POWER becomes dangerous when it begins to worship its own image. In today’s world, where wars, repression, displacement andpoliticalarrogance continue to endanger ordinary lives, this warning feels painfully alive. A state may claim strength......Politics

item.title: Sustainable future through permaculture
item.description: Across school yards once dominated by concrete and asphalt, a quiet yet powerful transformation is taking place. Raised beds of vegetables, herb spirals, compost bins, fruit trees and rainwater harvesting systems are gradually replacing barren spaces. They are not......

item.title: Debt trap of ‘concrete delta’
item.description: THE Bengal Delta is not a territory to be conquered; it is a geomorphic system shaped by the rhythm of the monsoon and the weight of Himalayan silt. Yet, for over a decade, this fluid landscape has been the subject of a grand experiment in......

item.title: Rhetoric v reality check
item.description: IF THERE were any ministry whose rampant mismanagement and spiral of failure had fuelled public despise against the BNP-Jamaat coalition government during its previous term from 2001 to 2006, the ministry of power would surely be at the top of......

item.title: A decision that may further endanger banks
item.description: The Bangladesh Bank’s decision to relax single borrower and large loan exposure limits appears a reckless retreat at a time when the banking sector is already standing on a fragile ground. The move effectively rewards the big borrowers that have......Bangladesh Travel Guide

item.title: Screen-dependency among children must be addressed
item.description: IT IS a worrying that children in urban areas are increasingly becoming dependent on digital devices, posing risks to their psychosocial development. An International Centre for Diarrhoeal Disease Research, Bangladesh study reports alarmingly high levels......

item.title: Zimbabwe tobacco hits new highs under smallholder contracts
item.description: Rows of green tobacco plants stretched towards the bush in a vast field of a crop that has rebounded to record levels in Zimbabwe, driven largely by smallholders contracted to Chinese firms....

item.title: Amid Russian invasion, Ukraine facing severe labour shortages
item.description: After fleeing Russia’s advancing army and resettling in the central industrial hub of Dnipro, Ukrainian worker Anatoliy Synkov had no trouble finding a job....

item.title: Rain looms as Bangladesh eye series win
item.description: Weather remains a concern, while both teams are expected to make changes to their playing elevens as Bangladesh look to secure a second successive series win over Pakistan in the second Test, starting at the Sylhet International Cricket Stadium today....

item.title: Alleged substandard seeds sparked concern among Meherpur farmers
item.description: FARMERS in Meherpur district expressed serious concerns over alleged poor-quality rice seeds from a government incentive programme, leading to widespread crop irregularities, financial losses, and uncertainty for this season’s boro production....

item.title: Livestock state minister calls for modernisation of national zoo
item.description: State minister for fisheries and livestock Sultan Salauddin Tuku in a surprise inspection oversaw the management standards and animal welfare of the Bangladesh National Zoo at Mirpur in Dhaka on Friday....Bangladesh Travel Guide

item.title: Content creator Kaarina Kaisar passes away
item.description: Popular content creator and media personality Kaarina Kaisar passed away while undergoing treatment at a hospital in Chennai in India on late Friday......

item.title: NCTB to revise textbooks to reflect true history: chair
item.description: The National Curriculum and Textbook Board has taken an initiative to correct long-standing distortions and one-sided historical narratives in primary and secondary school textbooks from the 2027 academic year to present the true history....

item.title: 8 fishermen, honey collectors abducted
item.description: Eight more fishermen and honey collectors have been abducted by a pirate group in the Satkhira range area of ​​the western Sundarbans on Thursday....

item.title: Two arrested over fish trader killing in Barishal
item.description: A fish trader was allegedly hacked to death in the Palashpur area of Barishal city early Friday after he was called out of his house for allegedly refusing to pay extortion money....

item.title: Sramik Majlis holds labour convention, places 7 demands
item.description: Politicians, labour leaders and professionals on Friday at a national labour convention in Dhaka called on the Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government to take steps to implement the rights of workers in the country....City & Local Guides

item.title: Two Saudi expatriates killed in road accident
item.description: Two Bangladeshi expatriates in Saudi Arabia were killed after a truck rammed the motorcycle they were riding on at Ashuganj upazila in Brahmanbaria on Friday afternoon....

item.title: Mock parliament demands trade deal with US reviewed
item.description: A mock parliament staged in the capital city on Friday demanded that the government must review the trade deal signed with the United States during the interim government....

item.title: Eid train ticket sales catch heavy traffic online
item.description: Advance train ticket sales online for May 25 journeys ahead of Eid-ul-Azha witnessed huge pressure on Friday as people rushed to secure seats for the peak travel day of the government-declared seven-day Eid holiday....

item.title: Algeria keen on investing in Bhola
item.description: Algerian ambassador to Bangladesh Abdelouahab Saidani has expressed strong interest in expanding investment cooperation in Bhola, particularly in gas-based industries, fisheries, livestock, and agro-processing sectors....

item.title: Last of 5-member family dies in N’ganj gas explosion
item.description: The last surviving member of a family of five, who sustained critical burn injuries in a gas explosion at their residence at Fatullah in Narayanganj, died while undergoing treatment at a hospital in Dhaka on Friday....

item.title: Special welfare card for cultural activists: minister
item.description: Cultural affairs minister Nitai Roy Chowdhury on Friday said the government will take steps to introduce a special welfare card for cultural activists to bring them under institutional support and social security coverage....

item.title: Volunteers to help ease traffic on Jamuna Bridge: Tuku
item.description: State minister for fisheries and livestock Sultan Salauddin Tuku on Friday said that the administration and volunteer teams will work together to prevent traffic jam on the Jamuna Bridge and Dhaka-Tangail highway ahead of the forthcoming Eid-ul-Adha....

item.title: Bogura City Corp officially declared
item.description: The government on Thursday night officially declared Bogura as the country’s 13th city corporation....

item.title: Worker dies in Bandarban soil collapse
item.description: A worker was killed after being buried under soil while cutting earth at a brick kiln in Lama upazila of Bandarban on Friday....

item.title: Youth shot by ‘teen gang’ in Dhaka
item.description: A 26-year-old man sustained bullet injuries after allegedly being shot by a ‘teen gang’ in the Shaheed Nagar area of Lalbagh in Dhaka Thursday night....Bangladesh Travel Guide

item.title: Teacher killed as scarf gets tangled in van wheel
item.description: A female madrassah teacher has been killed in Saturia upazila of Manikganj after her scarf got tangled in the wheel of a van....

item.title: 56,659 Hajj pilgrims reach Saudi Arabia
item.description: Over 56,000 Bangladeshi pilgrims have so far arrived in Saudi Arabia to perform Hajj this year, according to the ministry of religious affairs....

item.title: 5.74 lakh poor litigants get free legal aid thru NLASO
item.description: Over five lakh people across the country have received free legal advice and assistance under the government-funded legal aid programme from 2009 to April 2026, according to the National Legal Aid Services Organisation....

item.title: Repatriation only solution to Rohingya crisis: Bangladesh
item.description: Bangladesh has reaffirmed that the repatriation of displaced Rohingyas to their homeland Myanmar remains the only viable long-term solution to the crisis, calling for continued international support to ensure their safe return....City & Local Guides

item.title: PM to visit Chandpur today
item.description: People in Chandpur have raised several key development demands, including the establishment of an Export Processing Zone and a permanent embankment, ahead of the visit of prime minister Tarique Rahman to the district on Saturday....

item.title: Sylhet transport workers to strike from May 19
item.description: Transport workers in the Sylhet division have announced an indefinite work stoppage from 6:00am on May 19, demanding the withdrawal of what they say are false cases filed against several leaders of the Bangladesh Road Transport Workers Federation....

item.title: Attack on security compound kills 9 in Pakistan
item.description: Militants attacked a security compound in northwest Pakistan with an explosives-laden truck and gunfire, killing at least nine paramilitary officers, officials said Friday....

item.title: Kyiv in mourning after 24 killed Ukraine, Russia swap POWs
item.description: Ukraine’s capital Kyiv was in mourning on Friday after a Russian attack killed 24 people a day earlier, with president Volodymyr Zelensky denouncing Moscow’s ‘brutal terror’ and the two sides going ahead with an exchange of hundreds of captured soldiers....

item.title: 14 oil ships arrive in May, 5 to anchor soon
item.description: As part of the government’s efforts to ensure uninterrupted fuel supply in the country, 14 oil-carrying ships arrived in Chattogram port till May 15, while five more will anchor within the current month....

item.title: Martian valley shows signs of ancient water activity
item.description: The Mars Express mission of the European Space Agency has spotlighted Shalbatana Vallis, a vast Martian valley showing strong evidence of ancient water activity, volcanic processes and geological transformation....

item.title: 19 held over procession after ex-AL MP’s janaza
item.description: The police arrested 19 leaders and activists of the Bangladesh Awami League and its associate organisations after they allegedly brought out an anti-government procession following the namaz-e-janaza of former AL lawmaker Mosharraf Hossain in Chattogram on Thursday....Bangladesh Travel Guide

item.title: Devotees attacked at Mirpur shrine
item.description: A group of unidentified miscreants attacked devotees at a shrine in the Mirpur area of the capital Dhaka early Friday, prompting tensions among the devotees and a backlash on social media....Geographic Reference

item.description: North South University hosted an orientation programme for its new students at the NSU in Dhaka on Thursday....

item.description: The University of Liberal Arts Bangladesh organised an orientation for its newly admitted students of the summer 2026 semester on the ULAB campus in Dhaka on Thursday....

item.description: Aurpita Rani Paul, an MS student in the department of life sciences at Independent University, Bangladesh, has been awarded the National Science and Technology Fellowship 2025-2026 under the food and agriculturalsciencecategory. ...

item.title: Mango season arrives in Rajshahi with harvest of early varieties
item.description: The mango season formally arrived in Rajshahi on Friday as farmers officially began harvesting of early-ripening Guti varieties on the day from orchards across the district....

item.description: Speakers at a programme in Rajshahi on Friday called on BRAC to strengthen its grassroots engagement and address the region’s growing agricultural, environmental and social challenges....Diaspora Community Forum

item.description: Lisa Saha, principal at British Columbia School, has been honoured with the Global Icon award for educational leadership and women entrepreneurship in Bangladesh at a ceremony in Colombo, Sri Lanka recently. ...

item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard has arrested three members of a bandit gang named Karim Sharif during a special operation titled Operation Mangrove Shield in the Sundarban under Sharankhola in Bagerhat. ...Science

item.description: The Supreme Court of Bangladesh has provided one set of DLR, one set of ALR, one set of BLT and 23 reference books for the use of students of the law department of Dhaka University....

item.description: Professor Eqramul Hoque took charges as 16th vice-chancellor of the Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology on Friday....Bangladesh Travel Guide

item.title: Jamuna Bridge bottlenecks worry Eid travellers
item.description: Despite major progress in expanding the Dhaka-Rangpur national highway into a four-lane one, the unresolved bottleneck on the Jamuna Bridge remains a key concern for smooth journey to northern, north-western and southern districts, particularly during Eid....

item.title: 10th death anniv of journalist Sadeq Khan today
item.description: Today is the 10th anniversary of the death of language movement hero, freedom fighter and senior journalist Sadeq Khan....

item.title: Arakan Army continues to pick up fishermen from Bangladesh
item.description: Myanmar’s armed rebel group, the Arakan Army, continues to detain Bangladeshi and Rohingya fishermen from the Naf River and the Bay of Bengal, keeping them in custody in conflict-hit Myanmar....
